methods.description: Process for collecting and analyzing culture samples for pigment content using HPLC.

steps.description: Culture samples (10 ml) were collected and immediately filtered through GF/F Whatman filters (25 mm) and stored at -80°C until their analysis by HPLC.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and filter culture samples, store at -80°C

steps.description: Filters were extracted in 90% HPLC-grade acetone and ultrasonicated in an ice bath for 10 min, stored at -20°C for 24 h and prefiltered through a 0.45 μm Whatman nylon Puradisk filter before injection.
steps.searchable_summary: extract filters with acetone, ultrasonicate, store, and prefilter

steps.description: Pigments were separated using a reverse phase C-8 column following the method of Zapata et al. (2000).
steps.searchable_summary: separate pigments using reverse phase C-8 column

steps.description: External pigment standards were used for system calibration (DHI, Denmark).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate system using external pigment standards

steps.description: Samples were analyzed on a Waters 600 HPLC system, equipped with a Thermo Separation Products AS3000 sampler, a TSP Spectra 100 variable wavelength (fixed at 440 nm), a Waters 996 diode array (scanning 330-800 nm) and a Waters 470 scanning fluorescence detector. Data were collected and analyzed using the Waters Millennium 32 software package. Each sample was analyzed in duplicate.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using Waters 600 HPLC system and Millennium 32 software

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing microarray data to identify differentially expressed genes.

steps.description: Initial data analysis was performed as described in Shrestha et al. (2012).
steps.searchable_summary: perform initial data analysis as per Shrestha et al. (2012)

steps.description: Normalized and mapped probe intensities were then subjected to a two-pronged test for differential expression which included a false discovery rate (FDR) corrected student t-test statistic along with a log fold change cutoff of +/-1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform differential expression test with FDR correction and log fold change cutoff

steps.description: Two tailed unpaired t-tests were performed between time-points using the standard function within Microsoft Excel.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-tailed unpaired t-tests between time-points

steps.description: The FDR correction was performed via the method of Benjamini & Hochberg (1995) implemented by the R statistical software package.
steps.searchable_summary: apply FDR correction using Benjamini & Hochberg method in R

steps.description: Fold changes were calculated as the difference of any time-point from the initial 0hr time-point.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate fold changes from initial 0hr time-point

steps.description: Genes were considered to be differentially expressed if they had an FDR corrected p-value < .05 and a fold change greater or less than ± 1.
steps.searchable_summary: identify differentially expressed genes based on FDR p-value and fold change
